context: Defines validation rules for AODS corpus integrity, including schema, references, surface governance, stable-surface contracts, read-model freshness and watermarks, fixture and golden export conventions, root and module topology metadata, implementation evidence, implementation acceptance criteria, decision provenance, remediation guidance, severity/gating policy, reality checks, and AOP discipline.
assumptions: Validator has access to all files in corpus
JSON Schema draft-07 validation is available

sections.content: L1 MUST: manifest.json exists and validates against manifest.schema.json. Every modules[].path resolves inside corpus root. Every module file validates against module.schema.json. module_id matches manifest entry. No duplicate module IDs. No duplicate sid within module. Module schema permits section-first, artifact-first, or mixed modules, but every module must declare at least one section or artifact. Artifact decision_provenance, when declared, validates consumer_surface + basis + source_refs + evidence_status + consumption_gate shape. Read-model contract metadata, when declared, validates read_model.freshness snapshot_id + exported_at + source_watermark + staleness shape. boot_sequence and boot_by_role contain valid module IDs. boot_by_touch.match stays relative and corpus-contained. surface_pairs records match schema and keep human paths corpus-contained. human_generation, when declared, stays within the allowed opt-in contract: mode=deterministic, profile=overview|checklist, title optional. manifest-companion roles are runtime role profiles: id required; scope may be omitted; required_modules may be omitted when boot_by_role already carries runtime bindings. project_topology, when declared, validates root design_repo plus implementation_repos[] structure. module.meta.implementation and manifest.modules[].implementation, when declared, validate repo_id + paths + status + authority_surface shape. module.meta.implementation.evidence[], when declared, validates id + kind + locator + status + authority_surface shape. module.meta.implementation.acceptance_criteria[], when declared, validates id + surface_ref + requirement + check_type + blocking + status + authority_surface shape. manifest.modules[].implementation.evidence_summary, when declared, validates total + current + planned + stale + blocked counters. manifest.modules[].implementation.acceptance_summary, when declared, validates total + satisfied + planned + partial + waived + blocked + manual_review counters. control metadata, provenance summaries, runtime-contract summaries, redaction summaries, contract profile summaries, implementation linkage summaries, and schema-versioning summaries, when declared, validate on both manifest.modules[] and module.meta. surface-inventory entries keep relative paths and typed current|reserved|future states.

L2 MUST: deps[] resolve to manifest.modules[].id. section.refs[] resolve. section.artifact_refs[] resolve. role.required_modules[] resolve when declared. meta.capsule.routes_to resolve. boot_by_touch.load_modules[] resolve. surface_pairs.agent_primary and agent_supporting resolve. human_primary and human_supporting paths exist inside corpus root. Generated human_primary must re-render exactly from the declared deterministic generation contract. Paired human and agent surfaces must not disagree on conservatively extractable high-signal claims such as from-to deltas, must-include lists, timed actions, and execution preconditions. Claim-diff extraction reads agent_primary context, sections, and structured artifact semantics, and it compares full claim sets rather than treating one overlapping value as agreement. If a surface_pair declares exact boot_by_touch routes for both human_primary and agent_primary on the same intent, the human route must include the pair's semantic modules and must stay inside the paired agent route. Artifact-internal references also resolve: process-flow next targets, subprocess_ref, payload_schema, decision-tree node targets, decision_provenance source_refs, decision_provenance evidence_refs, decision_provenance summary_ref, topology endpoints, group node IDs, and mapping-table row width against declared columns. Cross-surface refs stay deterministic in v0.7: unresolved refs are blocking integrity failures, validators do not auto-fetch external corpora to guess targets, and stable authority must name the accountable ref owner instead of leaving target discovery implicit. Agent-consumable decisions with stable consumption_gate must have current evidence_status; stale, unresolved, or withheld evidence blocks stable decision consumption. Derived-summary or mixed decisions must declare summary_ref. module.meta.implementation.repo_id must resolve to manifest.project_topology.implementation_repos[].id when implementation linkage is declared. Current implementation linkage must declare at least one implementation evidence anchor and at least one implementation acceptance criterion. Evidence ids and acceptance criterion ids must be unique within the module. evidence-ref criteria must resolve to declared implementation evidence ids. Blocking criteria with blocked, partial, or waived status are failures. surface-inventory declarations keep relative paths even without reality mode. Under validate --reality, current entries resolve from the selected base: base=corpus -> corpus root; base=repo -> --repo-root when supplied, else corpus root. Reality mode rejects duplicate current declarations and distinguishes missing current paths from reserved or future declarations. When topology-aware implementation linkage is present, validate --reality also counts linked paths, missing paths, implementation evidence totals, stale evidence, checked evidence locators, missing evidence locators, and explicitly reports unchecked topology state when external repo roots cannot yet be resolved. Path-like implementation evidence locators are checked only when the linked repo root is resolvable; validators do not execute evidence commands. Circular module deps forbidden; circular section refs allowed with warning.

sections.topic: surface pair layer and capsule checks
sections.content: L3 SHOULD: every human-facing primary surface has paired agent_primary when corpus declares surface_pairs. boot_sequence starts with layer=root modules; capsule modules may follow root modules; detail and evidence should not lead boot. category=capsule <-> layer=capsule. category=capsule -> meta.capsule.{read_when, core_question, routes_to} present. sync_source=bidirectional warns until merge protocol exists, and the reference hook requires manual review when bidirectional pairs change. boot_by_touch should stay specific enough to narrow authoring loads instead of recreating full boot_by_role lists. Large corpora with empty boot_by_touch warn because missing file-scoped routes hide authoring cost and force broader loading. Hook layer rejects paired human-surface edits when sync_source=agent-primary and no paired agent module changed in the same change set, unless the changed human file is a valid deterministic regeneration; hook also rejects agent-only edits when sync_source=human-primary. manifest-only metadata edits do not satisfy semantic sync. shared_invariants, when declared on a surface_pair, must remain present in both agent_primary and human_primary texts after deterministic normalization of case, whitespace, and terminal punctuation; broader semantic drift remains the responsibility of claim-diff checks. Claim-diff checks stay conservative and only fire when validator can deterministically align both sides. If control metadata is declared, manifest.modules[].control and module.meta.control should match exactly, and sensor-only modules should not live in root or capsule layers. If provenance metadata is declared, manifest.modules[].provenance and module.meta.provenance should agree on memory_role + confidence. If runtime contracts are declared, manifest.modules[].runtime_contract and module.meta.runtime_contract should agree on side_effects + outputs, and side effects beyond none should expose telemetry, checkpoints, or outputs. If implementation linkage is declared, manifest.modules[].implementation and module.meta.implementation should agree on repo_id + paths + status + authority_surface + evidence_summary + acceptance_summary. Stale implementation evidence and manual-review acceptance criteria should warn so review debt is visible without blocking non-strict exploration. Topology-aware reality output should always report explicit unchecked_reason when linked external repositories remain unresolved from the selected repo root, and should warn only when project_topology is declared but no modules currently expose implementation linkage. Stable implementation-visible surfaces should have AODS authority before external, adapter-facing, exported, or agent-consumable use. Stable consumers should not depend on internal, experimental, local-only, or removed surfaces unless the dependency is explicitly experimental. surface-inventory reality checks are opt-in through validate --reality so existing corpora do not silently change behavior. base=repo only needs --repo-root when repo-scoped paths must resolve outside the corpus root. Module-like JSON files discovered under declared module directories should be registered in manifest.modules[]; otherwise validator warns that they are disk-only and unloaded. tokens_approx populated for all modules. Stable or frozen modules contain no TODO: or UNDEFINED: markers.

L2 MUST: if manifest.modules[].provenance or module.meta.provenance is declared, both sides stay present and memory_role + confidence match. L2 MUST: if manifest.modules[].runtime_contract or module.meta.runtime_contract is declared, both sides stay present and side_effects + outputs match. L2 MUST: runtime contracts with side effects other than none expose at least one observable anchor via telemetry, checkpoints, or outputs. L2 MUST: when surface-inventory artifacts run under validate --reality, current directories contain non-placeholder material and current file|directory kinds match the declared kind. These rules keep bootstrap routing aligned with deeper execution and prevent invisible side effects or stale current-state claims.

sections.topic: redaction contract and schema-versioning metadata checks
sections.content: L2 MUST: if manifest.modules[].redaction or module.meta.redaction is declared, both sides stay present and has_sensitive_payloads + redaction_posture match. L2 MUST: if module.meta.redaction.has_sensitive_payloads == true, then sensitive_classes and exposure_policy must be present. L2 MUST: if manifest.modules[].contract or module.meta.contract is declared, both sides stay present and profile matches. L2 MUST: if module.meta.contract.profile == read-model, then module.meta.contract.read_model.freshness must declare snapshot_id, exported_at, source_watermark, and staleness before stable consumption. L2 MUST: if artifact.decision_provenance is declared, source_refs, evidence_refs, and summary_ref must resolve to sections or artifacts; derived-summary or mixed decisions must name summary_ref; stable agent-consumable decisions require current evidence_status. L2 MUST: if manifest.modules[].implementation or module.meta.implementation is declared, both sides stay present and repo_id + paths + status + authority_surface + evidence_summary + acceptance_summary match. L2 MUST: implementation linkage requires manifest.project_topology.implementation_repos[] and the declared repo_id must resolve there. L2 MUST: current implementation linkage requires at least one evidence anchor and one acceptance criterion, duplicate evidence ids and duplicate criterion ids are rejected, evidence-ref criteria must resolve declared evidence ids, blocking unsatisfied criteria fail, and path-like evidence locators must exist when validate --reality can resolve the linked repository root. L3 SHOULD: manual-review criteria warn because they are reviewable but not automatically proven. L2 MUST: if manifest.modules[].schema_versioning or module.meta.schema_versioning is declared, both sides stay present and breaking_policy matches. These checks keep stable-surface contract posture visible at bootstrap instead of hiding it in deeper prose only.

Fixture and golden export conventions give drift and conformance work stable example names without turning benchmarks into mandatory conformance. Repository-level fixtures SHOULD live under fixtures/positive/<capability>/<case>/ for expected-pass cases and fixtures/negative/<rule>/<case>/ for expected-fail cases. Example-local fixture manifests MAY live next to an authoring example when the example is the authority surface. Golden exports SHOULD live under golden/exports/<target>/<case>/ or point at a generated example output when the export already exists. A fixture manifest uses aods_fixture_manifest_v=1 and convention=aods-fixture-golden-v1. Each fixture declares id, kind positive|negative, input.kind, input.path, expected_status pass|fail|warn, expected_rules, and optional golden_exports. Positive fixtures should use expected_status=pass and expected_rules=[]. Negative fixtures should name at least one expected validator rule. Each golden export declares id, kind, path, schema_version, update_command, and review_gate. Golden exports are outputs, not semantic authority; updates require rerunning the declared command, reviewing the diff, and running the smallest validation gate before repo-level validation. This convention does not require a full fixture runner, cross-repo fetch, or automatic golden update.

L4 SHOULD: no markdown syntax in content fields. No filler phrases. No conversational tone. No positional refs. Regex-based checks should ignore quoted anti-pattern examples when spec modules cite forbidden strings literally. Token density score > 3.5. No section content exceeds 2000 tokens. Capsule section text stays shorter than detail module section text for modules it routes to. Capsule-shorter-than-detail diagnostics should include approximate capsule and target token counts.

Validator outputs JSON: {corpus:str, timestamp:iso8601, strict:bool, accepted:bool, status:"pass"|"fail", levels:{L1,L2,L3,L4}, summary:{total_modules:int, total_sections:int, total_artifacts:int, total_tokens:int, total_surface_pairs:int, errors:int, warnings:int}, topology?:{linked_modules:int, unlinked_modules:int, checked_paths:int, missing_paths:int, evidence_total:int, modules_with_evidence:int, modules_without_evidence:int, stale_evidence:int, checked_evidence_locators:int, missing_evidence_locators:int, unchecked_reason?:str}}. In strict mode, warning-bearing levels report pass=false when warnings block the gate. topology is emitted only when validate --reality can see manifest.project_topology. issue = {level:str, module_id:str|null, sid:str|null, rule:str, message:str, severity_class?:str, remediation?:{action:str, gate:str, guidance:str}}. severity_class is optional because the current runtime still emits error/warning buckets first, while the richer gating taxonomy is specified separately in the validation severity policy. remediation is optional and appears only for findings with a standardized next action; it guides maintainer response but does not override the existing error/warning bucket.

v0.7 defines six governance severity classes: info, warning, strict-error, drift-blocking, security-blocking, and release-blocking. The current validator runtime still emits error and warning buckets, so these classes are a policy layer above the existing machine output rather than a full runtime rewrite. info is advisory only and does not block validate, strict, reality, or release. warning is advisory in normal validate output but fails strict compile or strict validate because strict mode already treats warnings as gate blockers. strict-error is the default failing class and must block compile, validate, strict, and any reality run that hits the finding. drift-blocking is a strict-error subclass for authority, pairing, invariant, topology, implementation evidence, or implementation acceptance drift findings; when present it must block strict, reality, and release. security-blocking is a strict-error subclass for redaction, sensitive leakage, auth, or trust-boundary violations; it must block validate, strict, reality, and release. release-blocking is the publication-facing class for version, adapter, packaging, or compatibility posture findings that may be tolerated during local iteration but must fail any release or export gate. Remediation gate labels identify the intended response path for a finding; they do not soften or bypass the runtime bucket. This keeps the current validator honest while giving future refs, adapter contracts, and release sensors one shared gate vocabulary.

artifacts.usage: Minimum fields for conventional fixture manifests and golden export references.

rows: fixture manifest schema version; current value is 1
convention identifier; current value is aods-fixture-golden-v1
human or automated gate required before accepting regenerated golden output
input authority type such as authoring-source, compiled-corpus, module, or repo-surface
relative path to the fixture input from the fixture manifest
expected validator rule ids; empty for positive pass fixtures
relative path to a generated output snapshot
command that regenerates the golden export

artifacts.usage: Minimal update workflow for golden exports.

steps.action: change the authoring source or semantic authority first
golden diff matches intended semantic change?
run the smallest declared validation gate, then repo-level validation when semantic surfaces changed

artifacts.usage: Lookup table for validation level semantics.

rows: missing manifest; invalid module schema; invalid surface_pairs shape; invalid human_generation enum

broken refs; broken routes_to; missing human path; generated human surface drift; paired claim conflict; control metadata mismatch; provenance summary mismatch; runtime contract mismatch; redaction/contract/implementation/schema-versioning mirror mismatch; missing read-model freshness; invalid implementation repo refs; missing implementation evidence; missing acceptance criteria; missing evidence locators; invalid acceptance evidence refs; invisible side effects; sensor declared in root/capsule; invalid process-flow targets; invalid topology endpoints; mapping-table row mismatch; missing or placeholder-only current surfaces in reality mode
capsule missing meta.capsule; boot order starts with detail; missing tokens_approx; unregistered module-like JSON on disk; topology declared without any implementation-linked modules
markdown in content; filler prose; oversized section

artifacts.usage: Governance severity classes layered above the current validator error/warning buckets.

rows: purely advisory note; no current built-in gate behavior
visible advisory that blocks only when strict mode is requested
default failing class for validate and compile acceptance
strict-error subclass for authority, sync, invariant, or topology drift
strict-error subclass for trust-boundary or sensitive-data violations
must fail release or export gates even if local iteration treats it more softly

artifacts.usage: Minimal relation between severity classes and v0.7 gates.

rows: fail when the finding is in scope
fail once release-quality posture is expected
fail when release depends on reality evidence

artifacts.usage: Standard next-action guidance for validation findings that expose implementation or contract drift.

rows: stable surface consumed without AODS authority
declare the authority entry, owner, lifecycle, contract profile, and redaction posture before stable consumption
implementation-visible surface not ready for stable consumption
downgrade exposure until authority and contract completeness are available
stable consumer depends on non-authorized or removed surface
remove the dependency or migrate it to an authorized replacement
current implementation linkage lacks evidence anchors
declare module.meta.implementation.evidence[] with reviewable proof anchors
implementation evidence ids are duplicated within a module
rename or merge duplicate evidence ids so each proof anchor has one stable identity
path-like evidence locator is missing under validate --reality
restore the evidence path or update the locator before treating implementation status as current
current implementation linkage lacks contract acceptance criteria
declare module.meta.implementation.acceptance_criteria[] linking contract requirements to evidence, validator rules, fixtures, or manual review
implementation acceptance criteria ids are duplicated within a module
rename or merge duplicate criteria ids so each contract requirement has one stable identity
evidence-ref acceptance criterion has no evidence_refs entries
add at least one evidence_refs entry so the requirement is tied to proof
point evidence_refs at declared evidence ids or add the missing evidence anchor
blocking acceptance criterion is blocked, partial, or waived
make the criterion satisfied or record an explicit waiver/remediation before stable consumption
acceptance criterion relies on manual review
prefer automated evidence where possible and keep the manual review anchor current when automation is not available
decision provenance references an unknown source
point source_refs at declared sections or artifacts that authorize the decision
decision provenance references an unknown evidence anchor
point evidence_refs at declared sections or artifacts that provide proof anchors
decision provenance references an unknown derived summary
point summary_ref at the section or artifact that owns the derived summary
derived-summary or mixed decision provenance lacks summary_ref
declare summary_ref so agents can locate the synthesis boundary
stable agent-consumable decision has stale, unresolved, or withheld evidence
refresh the evidence or change consumption_gate to review-required, blocked, or experimental
stable read-model contract lacks snapshot freshness and source watermark metadata
declare module.meta.contract.read_model.freshness with snapshot_id, exported_at, source_watermark, and staleness before stable consumption

artifacts.usage: Surface-pair, layer, and capsule validation rules.

rules.action: L1 error: agent_primary missing
L1 error: human_primary must stay inside corpus root
L1 error: boot_by_touch match must stay inside corpus root
L2 error: capsule metadata missing
L2 error: capsule routes_to unresolved
L2 error: declared shared_invariants missing from paired surfaces after deterministic normalization
L2 error: paired surfaces contain contradictory high-signal claims
L2 error: generated human surfaces require agent-primary sync
L2 error: generated human surface is out of sync with deterministic render

rules.condition: module.control.declared == true AND mirror_match(module.control, manifest.modules[].control) == false
rules.action: L2 error: manifest.modules[].control must match module.meta.control

rules.condition: module.provenance.declared == true AND mirror_match(module.provenance.summary, manifest.modules[].provenance) == false
rules.action: L2 error: manifest.modules[].provenance must match module.meta.provenance summary fields
L2 error: manifest.modules[].runtime_contract must match module.meta.runtime_contract summary fields
L2 error: runtime contracts with side effects must declare telemetry, checkpoints, or outputs

rules.condition: module.redaction.declared == true AND mirror_match(module.redaction.summary, manifest.modules[].redaction) == false
rules.action: L2 error: manifest.modules[].redaction must match module.meta.redaction summary fields
L2 error: sensitive modules must declare sensitive_classes and exposure_policy

rules.condition: module.contract.declared == true AND mirror_match(module.contract.summary, manifest.modules[].contract) == false
rules.action: L2 error: manifest.modules[].contract must match module.meta.contract summary fields
L2 error: stable read-model contracts must declare freshness and watermark metadata
L2 error: manifest.modules[].schema_versioning must match module.meta.schema_versioning summary fields
L2 error: manifest.modules[].implementation must match module.meta.implementation summary fields
L2 error: project_topology.implementation_repos[] ids must be unique
L2 error: module.meta.implementation.repo_id must resolve to manifest.project_topology.implementation_repos[]

rules.condition: artifact.decision_provenance exists AND any source_refs target is unresolved
rules.action: L2 error: decision provenance source refs must resolve to sections or artifacts

rules.condition: artifact.decision_provenance exists AND any evidence_refs target is unresolved
rules.action: L2 error: decision provenance evidence refs must resolve to sections or artifacts

rules.condition: artifact.decision_provenance.summary_ref exists AND summary_ref target is unresolved
rules.action: L2 error: decision provenance summary_ref must resolve to a section or artifact

rules.condition: artifact.decision_provenance.basis is derived-summary or mixed AND summary_ref is missing
rules.action: L2 error: derived decision provenance must identify the summary owner

rules.condition: artifact.decision_provenance.consumer_surface == agent-consumable AND consumption_gate == stable AND evidence_status IN stale, unresolved, withheld
rules.action: L2 error: stable agent-consumable decisions require current evidence posture
L2 error: current surfaces must exist in the selected corpus or repo root
L2 error: current directories must contain non-placeholder material
L2 error: current surfaces must not be declared twice for the same base/path/kind
L3 warning: stable surfaces should declare AODS authority before external consumption

rules.condition: consumer.lifecycle == 'stable' AND dependency.lifecycle IN ['internal','experimental','local-only','removed'] AND dependency.experimental_dependency != true
rules.action: L3 warning: stable consumers should not depend on non-stable surfaces without explicit experimental marking
L2 error: sensor-only modules must not live in root or capsule layers
L3 warning: boot should start with root layer
L2 error: boot_by_touch load_modules unresolved
L3 warning: large corpora should add file-scoped boot_by_touch routes

rules.condition: reality_mode == true AND manifest.project_topology exists AND topology_linked_modules == 0
rules.action: L3 warning: topology-aware reality summary is partial because no modules currently expose implementation linkage
L3 warning: evidence should not participate in initial boot
L3 warning: bidirectional sync remains experimental until merge protocol exists

changelog.delta: Initial validation specification.
Added surface-pair, layer, capsule, and boot-order validation rules plus total_surface_pairs in validation output.
Clarified capsule layer/category identity and added explicit bidirectional-sync warning semantics.
Hardened corpus-contained path rules and clarified that manifest-only edits do not satisfy agent-primary hook sync.
Added shared_invariants surface-pair validation so declared semantic anchors become built-in detectable drift failures.
Added conservative paired-surface claim-conflict validation for deterministic high-signal semantic contradictions.
Added control metadata mirroring and sensor-layer restrictions for the explicit control taxonomy.
Added provenance summary mirroring and runtime-contract observability rules so bootstrap metadata stays aligned with deeper execution contracts.
Added opt-in surface-inventory reality validation for current path existence, kind checks, duplicate current declarations, and placeholder-only directory detection.
Validation JSON now exposes strict/accepted/status gate fields, and strict mode marks warning-bearing levels as failing when warnings block acceptance.
Added validation posture for stable-surface authority and non-stable dependency warnings.
Clarified capsule-shorter-than-detail diagnostics with approximate compared token counts.
Added advisory warning for empty boot_by_touch in larger corpora.
Added stable-surface contract metadata validation for redaction, contract profiles, and schema versioning summaries.
Added the v0.7 severity taxonomy and minimal gate matrix without rewriting current validator runtime buckets.
Added module implementation linkage mirror/ref checks and topology-aware reality summary output semantics.
Added duplicate implementation repository id validation for topology identity safety.
Added implementation evidence validation, manifest evidence summary matching, and reality checks for path-like evidence locators.
Added implementation acceptance criteria validation and manifest acceptance summary matching.
Added optional validation issue remediation guidance for implementation and contract drift findings.
Added validation guidance for artifact-level decision provenance refs and stable agent-consumable decision evidence posture.
Added validation guidance and remediation action for stable read-model freshness and watermark metadata.
Added fixture and golden export conventions for positive and negative examples, golden update review, and example-local fixture manifests.
